channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Future of AI: Navigating Uncertainty and Finding Practical Solutions
item.description: The future of AI is bright, yet its continuous evolution and uncertain regulatory environment cloud its reality for many businesses.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s plan to replace government workers with AI could create chaos
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk’s plan to cut $2 trillion in US government spending apparently includes replacing tens of thousands of federal workers with AI, but the move could produce disastrous results, several AI experts say.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft updates VS Code C# Dev Kit extension
item.description: Microsoft announced updates to its C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension, including enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and for debugging and testing Blazor web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft unveils company's first quantum computing chip
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday revealed its first quantum computing chip, Majorana 1, stating it will speed up the development of quantum computers able to solve "meaningful, industrial-scale problems in years, not decades.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: What does the 'e' in iPhone 16e mean? Here are my best and most unexpected ideas
item.description: While the SE in the iPhone SE stood for 'Special Edition," Apple isn't being as clear with what the "e" in iPhone 16e stands for.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: This startup wants to be Shopify for indie filmmakers
item.description: A new startup is leaning on the power of recommendations to spread the word about indie films. Olyn offers a direct-to-consumer platform for film and video distribution, enabling filmmakers to retain up to 90% of their revenue.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fortinet speeds threat detection with improved FortiAnalyzer
item.description: Fortinet has reinforced its FortiAnalyzer security analytics and log management platform with capabilities that enhance enterprise threat detection and automate incident response. FortiAnalyzer securely collects logs from Fortinet devices such as FortiGate firewalls and endpoint security agents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Everything new on Hulu in March 2025 – including Sean Baker's Oscar-nominated drama Anora
item.description: Anora is just one of the new movies coming to Hulu in March 2025. Here's the full list of the titles coming to the platform next month.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia's RTX 5070 Ti looks like the go-to GPU this generation, if you can find it at retail price - benchmarks and reviews suggest it's almost as good as the RTX 5080
item.description: The RTX 5070 Ti is shaping up to be a great GPU, but inflated prices may spoil its potential success.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone 16e renews Apple’s push into budget smartphone market
item.description: Apple has faced increased competition from Google and Samsung, in the budget smartphone market and in the AI space
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft’s quantum ambition: One million qubits in the palm of your hand
item.description: Microsoft announced a breakthrough in quantum computing today, unveiling a new kind of quantum processing unit, using a new type of material, to create what it says is a “radically different type of qubit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Netflix is getting its first-ever English medical procedural series and a historical drama that looks like a Spanish Bridgerton
item.description: Netflix has announced its first English-language medical procedural series and a Spanish period drama that looks straight out of Bridgerton.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Apple TV+ releases a gritty new crime drama trailer for Dope Thief that looks like a stylish version of The Wire
item.description: Apple TV+'s violent Philadelphia-set show has snagged the legendary Hollywood helmer and will start airing in March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: National Science Foundation cuts 10 percent of staff
item.description: Nearly 170 employees at the National Science Foundation (NSF) who were still on probation were fired by the Trump administration on Tuesday, union officials told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk doesn’t work for DOGE, says White House as battle for government servers intensifies
item.description: Attempts to challenge the power of Elon Musk and his DOGE team to close down government departments have hit an unexpected complication: according to the White House, the entrepreneur is not even in charge of the operation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump, Musk say media trying to drive them apart
item.description: President Trump and Elon Musk, one of his top allies, argued that the mainstream media is trying to drive a wedge between them and stymie the president's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Don't let AI phantom hackers drain your bank account
item.description: Attackers use caller ID spoofing and AI voices to steal money. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides seven ways to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump and EU Commission set course for confrontation on big tech
item.description: width="2193" height="1234" sizes="(max-width: 2193px) 100vw, 2193px">With the second Trump administration, very different cultures are once again clashing in the transatlantic relationship. rawf8 – shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Serverless was never a cure-all
item.description: An obituary for serverless computing might read something like this: In loving memory of serverless computing, born from the fervent hopes of developers and cloud architects alike.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Net neutrality under Trump? Not so neutral
item.description: Even before President Donald J. Trump returned to office last month, net neutrality took a punch to the jaw. On Jan. 2, the US Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit struck down the Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) net neutrality rules. Oh well, it was nice while it lasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Large language models: The foundations of generative AI
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) such as GPT, Bard, and Llama have caught the public’s imagination and garnered a wide variety of reactions. They are also expected to grow dramatically in the coming years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mac users beware: AI-powered malware threats are on the rise
item.description: Even Apple products are vulnerable to cybercriminals, and a new report suggests the owners of Mac products will have to be more vigilant this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewall bug being exploited by threat actors: Report
item.description: Admins with firewalls from Palo Alto Networks should make sure the devices are fully patched and the management interface blocked from open internet access after the discovery this week of a zero-day login authentication bypass in the PAN-OS operating system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps
item.description: Creating a forward-thinking framework that keeps sight of what came before makes disruption and digital maturity possible, says Leidos CIO Alexandra Guenther. The post How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
